description_long: This is a rectangular stone block with a rough, irregular surface and slightly rounded edges, suggesting natural weathering. The block is predominantly light gray with patches of yellowish-green discoloration, likely from moss or lichen growth, especially along one edge. Its surface texture is uneven, with visible pitting and minor indentations, and one end features two pronounced, rounded protrusions. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cracks or major fractures. The coloration and surface details indicate it has been exposed to the elements for some time, contributing to its aged appearance.
description: A weathered, rectangular stone block with rough, pitted surfaces and patches of yellow-green moss or lichen. The block is light gray and features rounded edges and minor surface indentations.
description_view_0: The main face is visible, showing a broad, flat surface with yellow-green discoloration along the upper edge and a generally light gray color.
description_view_1: This end view highlights two rounded protrusions and a small cavity, with more concentrated moss or lichen growth on the left side.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is mostly uniform in color and texture, with less visible discoloration and a smoother appearance compared to the other side.
description_view_3: The rear end view shows a rough, slightly sloped surface with minimal moss and a more pronounced weathered texture.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted soil or sand
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible moss or lichen growth along one edge and some surface pitting, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure and natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or loosely aligned with other blocks
